tool.name: detect_waf
tool.description:
Detect Web Application Firewall (WAF) presence by fingerprinting HTTP responses.
This tool analyzes HTTP response headers, cookies, and body content to identify WAF vendors. It uses passive fingerprinting and optionally sends benign test requests to trigger WAF responses.
PASSIVE RECONNAISSANCE: This tool makes HTTP requests but does NOT send any malicious payloads. It only uses response fingerprinting to identify WAFs.
Supported WAFs: - Cloudflare (headers: cf-ray, cookies: __cf_bm) - Akamai Kona Site Defender (cookies: _abck, ak_bmsc) - AWS WAF (headers: x-amzn-requestid) - Imperva Incapsula (cookies: visid_incap, incap_ses) - Sucuri CloudProxy (headers: x-sucuri-id) - F5 BIG-IP ASM (cookies: BIGipServer) - ModSecurity (body patterns) - FortiWeb, Barracuda, Wordfence, Azure WAF, and more
Use this tool BEFORE attempting any penetration testing to understand what defenses are in place.

parameters.parameter: If true, send additional requests with benign test strings that might trigger WAF responses (e.g., SQLi patterns in User-Agent). Default: false. This helps identify WAFs that only block on suspicious input.
Include detailed match information (which headers/cookies matched). Default: false.

tool.returns: JSON object containing: - success: Whether detection succeeded - url: The analyzed URL - waf_detected: Boolean indicating if a WAF was detected - wafs: List of detected WAFs with confidence scores (0-1) - primary_waf: The most likely WAF (name, vendor, confidence%, waf_id) - headers_analyzed: Key headers that were checked - cookies_found: Cookies set by the server - recommendations: Suggested next steps - message: Human-readable status
tool.workflow: 1. Run detect_waf() on target URL 2. If WAF detected, use get_waf_evasion_strategies() with the waf_id 3. Consider using crtsh_search() or shodan_search() to find origin IP 4. Adjust testing approach based on WAF capabilities

tool.name: get_waf_evasion_strategies
tool.description:
Get WAF evasion strategies and bypass techniques for a specific WAF.
This tool provides educational information about WAF bypass techniques. Use this after detect_waf() identifies the target's WAF.
IMPORTANT: These techniques are for AUTHORIZED SECURITY TESTING ONLY. Attempting to bypass security controls without authorization is illegal.
Available strategy categories: - Origin IP Discovery: Finding the real server IP behind CDN/WAF - Encoding Techniques: URL encoding, unicode, case variation - Browser Emulation: Mimicking legitimate browser behavior - Protocol-Level: HTTP smuggling, method override, chunked encoding - Payload Manipulation: Splitting, concatenation, comments
Supported WAFs with detailed strategies: - cloudflare: Origin discovery, rate limit bypass, encoding - akamai: Browser emulation, sensor handling, timing - aws_waf: Rule fingerprinting, encoding bypass - imperva_incapsula: Cookie chain, origin discovery - modsecurity: CRS bypass, paranoia level detection - f5_big_ip: Support ID analysis - sucuri: Origin discovery, default rule bypass - generic: Universal techniques applicable to any WAF

parameters.parameter: WAF identifier from detect_waf() result (e.g., "cloudflare", "akamai"). Use "generic" for universal bypass techniques.
Alternative: search by WAF name (e.g., "Cloudflare", "AWS WAF"). Will be converted to waf_id.

tool.returns: JSON object containing: - success: Whether lookup succeeded - waf: WAF name and vendor information - general_info: Overview of the WAF and its defenses - strategies: List of evasion strategies, each with: - name: Strategy name - description: What the strategy does - techniques: Specific techniques to try - effectiveness: Expected success rate (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) - limitations: Known limitations and caveats - available_wafs: List of WAFs with strategies (if no waf_id provided)
tool.workflow: 1. First run detect_waf() to identify the WAF 2. Use the waf_id from detect_waf() result 3. Review strategies and limitations 4. Also get generic strategies: get_waf_evasion_strategies(waf_id="generic") 5. Prioritize origin discovery if WAF is strong
